summary: Add Tailwind dark: variants to AddTodoModal and EditTodoModal so the modal overlay, panel, heading, inputs, textarea, and date field match the app's existing dark-mode styling (darkMode: 'media' is already configured and used in pages/index.tsx and styles/globals.css). Also apply small responsive (w-full max-w-md mx-4) and accessibility (role=dialog, aria-modal, focus rings) tweaks. No API or behavior changes.

required_changes.change: Update Tailwind classes only. Overlay: append 'dark:bg-black dark:bg-opacity-60'. Modal panel: replace 'border w-96 ... bg-white' with 'border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700 w-full max-w-md mx-4 shadow-lg rounded-md bg-white dark:bg-gray-900' and add attributes role="dialog" aria-modal="true". Heading: append 'dark:text-gray-100'. Each input/textarea/date: replace 'border rounded text-gray-900 placeholder-gray-500' with 'border border-gray-300 dark:border-gray-700 rounded bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-100 placeholder-gray-500 dark:placeholder-gray-400 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500'. Buttons: append 'focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-400'. Do not modify component props, state, validation, button labels, or onAdd/onClose behavior.
required_changes.reason: The current modal is hardcoded to bg-white with light text, ignoring the app-wide dark-mode tokens already used in pages/index.tsx and styles/globals.css. This produces the bright/white background described in the issue.
acceptance_criteria: Under prefers-color-scheme: dark, the modal panel renders with bg-gray-900 and gray-100 text.
Under light mode, the modal renders white background with dark text (no visual regression).
Inputs, textarea, and date field have dark backgrounds (gray-800) and readable placeholder text (gray-400) in dark mode.
Modal panel has role="dialog" and aria-modal="true".
Modal panel uses 'w-full max-w-md mx-4' and does not overflow on viewports narrower than ~430px.
Component props, exported function signature, and onAdd/onClose behavior are unchanged.

required_changes.change: Apply the same class changes as AddTodoModal so Add and Edit are visually identical: dark: variants on overlay, panel, heading, and each input/textarea/date; responsive width 'w-full max-w-md mx-4'; focus rings; role="dialog" and aria-modal="true". Do not modify props (todo, onSave, onClose), state initialization from todo, validation logic, or button labels.
required_changes.reason: EditTodoModal duplicates AddTodoModal's light-only styling and must be updated in lockstep so the add and edit experiences look identical.
acceptance_criteria: Edit modal matches Add modal in colors, borders, focus styles, and spacing in both light and dark mode.
Existing onSave/onClose behavior and form validation are unchanged.
Modal panel has role="dialog" and aria-modal="true".
Initial values for title/details/deadline still come from props.todo.

out_of_scope: Extracting a shared <Modal /> component between AddTodoModal and EditTodoModal.
Implementing focus trap, Esc-to-close, or click-outside-to-close.
Switching tailwind darkMode from 'media' to 'class' or adding a manual theme toggle.
Restyling TodoItem, pages/index.tsx, or other unrelated components.
Adding automated visual or e2e tests.
Changing component APIs or button labels.
risks: darkMode is 'media', so reviewers without OS-level dark mode will not see the dark variant without toggling system theme or temporarily flipping tailwind.config.js to 'class'.
Native <input type="date"> calendar picker chrome is browser-controlled and may still appear light on some browsers; styling is best-effort.
Tailwind already scans components/**, so new dark: classes will be picked up without config changes.
open_questions: Should darkMode be switched from 'media' to 'class' with a user-facing toggle? Deferred — out of scope for this slice.
